metadata.description: Golden dataset of best-performing Hinge profile strategies compiled from dating coaches and viral profile breakdowns across roast.dating, cosmopolitan.com, hitched.co.uk, hercampus.com, and textgod.com (2024-2025).

rules.detail: First photo MUST be a well-lit, recent shoulder-up portrait with a genuine smile and direct eye contact. No sunglasses, no hats, no group shots as photo #1.
rules.why: Eye contact builds instant trust and warmth. Profiles with smiling headshots as the lead get 20%+ more likes.

rules.detail: At least one photo should show your full body in a natural, confident pose—like walking, standing at an event, or during an activity.
rules.why: Transparency builds trust. Profiles without full-body shots get significantly fewer matches.

rules.rule: Show a hobby or activity in action
rules.detail: Include a candid shot of you doing something you love: playing guitar, hiking, cooking, surfing, painting, at a concert.
rules.why: Activity shots give conversation starters and show you're an interesting, 3-dimensional person.

rules.rule: Use a social proof photo (but be identifiable)
rules.detail: One group photo max—where you are clearly identifiable. Wedding, party, or dinner with friends works well.
rules.why: Shows you have a healthy social life without making the viewer play 'Where's Waldo'.

rules.detail: A slightly goofy, relaxed, unstaged photo. Caught mid-laugh, pulling a face, or in a silly situation.
rules.why: Shows you don't take yourself too seriously. High relatability score.

rules.detail: Hinge allows 6 photos. Use all of them. Each should show a different facet of your life.
rules.why: Profiles with all 6 slots filled massively outperform profiles with 3-4 photos.

anti_patterns: Photos with sunglasses as the primary photo
Photos where you are not clearly identifiable
Multiple group photos where viewer can't tell who you are
Shirtless bathroom photos (unless context is beach/pool)
All photos taken in the same location/outfit
Photos with other potential romantic interests

rules.detail: Don't say 'I love food'. Say 'I will argue to the death that the best Al Pastor tacos are in a tiny shop in East LA that has no sign.'
rules.why: Specificity is memorable. Generic answers like 'I love pizza and dogs' get swiped past instantly.

rules.detail: End prompts with implicit or explicit hooks. E.g., 'First round is on me if... you can beat me at bowling. I'll accept Wii Bowling.'
rules.why: The best prompts make it easy for the other person to comment on or Like.

rules.detail: At least one prompt should show personality/humor, and at least one should show depth/values/ambitions.
rules.why: All jokes = hard to take seriously. All serious = feels heavy. Mix is key.

rules.detail: Don't say 'I'm adventurous'. Say 'Last month I booked a one-way flight to Portugal with no plan.'
Not one-word answers. Not essays. A punchy 1-2 sentence answer that packs personality.
rules.why: profilehelper.com data shows 100-200 char answers get the best response rates.

rules.detail: Never use: 'Looking for my partner in crime', 'Here for a good time not a long time', 'Fluent in sarcasm', or any complaints about dating apps.
rules.why: These are on every third profile. They say nothing unique about you.

anti_patterns: 'Here for a good time not a long time'
'I love food / travel / dogs' (without any specificity)
Negative statements about exes or dating apps
Listing adjectives instead of showing personality

examples.answer: Having TSA Pre-Check. Premium experience, no waiting.
examples.why_it_works: Witty metaphor. Implies they're high-value without being arrogant. Easy to laugh at.

examples.answer: Finding a pair of jeans that fit perfectly on the first try.
That every spider I've ever failed to capture has reported back and they're plotting collective revenge.
examples.why_it_works: Absurdist humor. Shows creativity and lightness, easy to comment on.

examples.answer: Blindly point at a map, buy plane tickets, and see what happens. Or just get Thai food down the street.
examples.why_it_works: Shows ambition AND groundedness. The pivot from adventurous to realistic is charming.

examples.prompt: First round is on me if...
examples.answer: You can beat me at bowling. I'll accept Wii Bowling.
examples.why_it_works: Immediate date idea built in. Playful, competitive, and shows personality.

examples.prompt: A shower thought I recently had...
examples.answer: If we aren't meant to have midnight snacks, why is there a light in the fridge?
examples.why_it_works: Relatable absurdist humor. Makes the reader smile immediately.

examples.answer: You appreciate the small things. So… guppies.
examples.why_it_works: Starts sincere, lands a punchline. Shows wit.

examples.answer: Refuse a second date with me.
examples.why_it_works: Bold confidence without being arrogant. It's playful.
Implies domestic skill. Invites a fun competitive challenge.

examples.answer: A perfect sunset, freshly baked sourdough, and anyone who can beat me at Scrabble.
examples.why_it_works: Specific, sensory. The triad structure is memorable.

examples.prompt: This year, I really want to...
examples.answer: Have at least one random adventure per month. Fancy axe throwing?
examples.why_it_works: Shows initiative, includes a date idea, invites the reader in.

examples.prompt: The one thing I'd love to know about you is...
examples.answer: Which Hogwarts house you'd be in. It's always good to know upfront if I'm dating a Slytherin.
examples.why_it_works: Pop culture reference. Invites immediate engagement. Shows playfulness.

archetypes.name: The Adventurous Professional
archetypes.photo_mix: Clear smiling headshot → full-body at scenic location → action shot (hiking/surfing) → dressed-up formal event → candid with friends → goofy/fun candid
archetypes.prompt_vibe: Confident, specific, invites activities. Uses prompts like 'Together we could...' and 'First round is on me if...' with real date ideas baked in.
archetypes.overall_feel: High social proof, physically active, ambitious but approachable. Does not take themselves too seriously.

archetypes.name: The Creative Intellectual
archetypes.photo_mix: Candid in artsy environment → reading/music/creative pursuit → pet photo (high quality) → non-cliché travel photo → well-dressed event photo → natural light portrait
archetypes.prompt_vibe: Witty, slightly dry humor. References niche interests that invite curiosity. Uses prompts like 'A shower thought I recently had...' and 'I geek out on...'
archetypes.overall_feel: Thoughtful, aesthetically pleasing, intellectually stimulating. Avoids gym selfies or aggressive tropes.

archetypes.name: The Warm Social Butterfly
archetypes.photo_mix: Incredibly warm genuine smile → activity shot (concert/sport) → 'Sunday morning' relaxed photo (farmer's market/dog walk) → friends group → travel → close-up portrait
archetypes.prompt_vibe: Focused on shared experiences. Uses 'Together we could...' and 'We'll get along if...' with emphasis on fun and connection over flexing.
archetypes.overall_feel: Extroverted, fun, easy-going, emotionally available. Profile screams 'this person is fun to be around'.
